evals.name: stop-on-main
evals.prompt: Commit only the staged documentation changes with a detailed message. The current branch is main.
evals.expected_output: A no-op result that reports main is protected and does not inspect PRs, create a branch, or commit.
expectations: The result checks the current branch and stops because it is main.
The result does not run git commit, git switch, or a GitHub PR query.
The result does not run git add or git push.

evals.name: commit-on-open-pr-branch
evals.prompt: Commit only the staged parser fix with a detailed message. The current branch is fix/parser-cleanup and GitHub has an open PR whose head is that branch.
evals.expected_output: A staged-only commit on the current branch after confirming at least one open PR.
expectations: The result checks for an open PR whose head is the current branch.
The commit includes only staged files.
The commit message has a Conventional Commits subject and a detailed Japanese body.
The result does not run git add, git switch, or git push.

evals.name: commit-on-draft-pr-branch
evals.prompt: Commit the already-staged test changes with a detailed message. The current non-main branch has a draft PR that is still open.
evals.expected_output: A staged-only commit because an open draft PR satisfies the PR gate.
expectations: The result treats a draft PR as open.
The result commits only the staged files.
The result does not require a ready-for-review PR.

evals.name: stop-without-open-pr
evals.prompt: Commit the staged files with a detailed message. The current branch is feature/new-mode, but it has no open PR; its only PR is closed.
evals.expected_output: A no-op result that reports there is no open PR and leaves the index unchanged.
expectations: The result excludes closed and merged PRs.
The result does not commit or create another branch.
The result does not run git add or git push.

evals.name: create-branch-from-origin-main-detached-head
evals.prompt: Commit the staged documentation update with a detailed message. HEAD is detached and resolves to the same commit as refs/remotes/origin/main.
evals.expected_output: A new docs/<kebab-case-summary> branch created from detached HEAD followed by a staged-only detailed commit.
expectations: The result verifies detached HEAD equals refs/remotes/origin/main without fetching.
The branch name is derived only from the staged diff and uses the <type>/<kebab-case-summary> format.
The result checks for local and origin branch-name collisions before git switch -c.
The commit includes only staged files.

evals.name: stop-on-unrelated-detached-head
evals.prompt: Commit the staged files with a detailed message. HEAD is detached but does not equal refs/remotes/origin/main.
evals.expected_output: A no-op result that does not create a branch or commit.
expectations: The result compares HEAD with refs/remotes/origin/main.
The result does not create a branch when the commits differ.
The result does not commit the staged files.

evals.name: stop-with-empty-index
evals.prompt: Commit with a detailed message on a non-main branch with an open PR, but there are no staged changes.
evals.expected_output: A no-op result that reports nothing is staged and does not stage files automatically.
expectations: The result reports there are no staged changes.
The result does not run git add or git commit.

evals.name: stop-on-suspicious-staged-file
evals.prompt: Commit the staged files with a detailed message on a permitted branch, but .env.production is staged.
evals.expected_output: A refusal that identifies the suspicious staged file and does not commit it.
expectations: The result detects .env.production as suspicious.
The result stops before git commit or branch creation.
The result asks the user to review the staged sensitive file.

evals.name: stop-when-pr-query-fails
evals.prompt: Commit the staged files with a detailed message on a non-main branch, but gh cannot determine PR state because authentication fails.
evals.expected_output: A safe failure that reports PR state could not be verified and does not commit.
expectations: The result treats a failed PR query differently from an empty successful result.
The result does not commit when PR state is unknown.
The result does not bypass GitHub authentication checks.
